mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy exceeding 55 percent over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous peaks in retail sales and housing starts indicate demand exhaustion and inventory overhang, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal accuracy drops below 50 percent over a 5-year rolling window, or forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of co-peak observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing maximums, supporting short-term earnings momentum and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of rising INDPRO observations within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation crossing above 3.5 percent year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of threshold-crossing instances, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline benchmark with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 3-year walk-forward test, or accuracy falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, creating a persistent upward drift in equity prices that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal predicts negative returns in over 55 percent of positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with falling retail sales during a post-shock recovery phase signals a demand-driven supply contraction, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal loses predictive accuracy when both series decline simultaneously, or forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered observations within the specified regime.
